intro: Craspedorhachis texana, also known as Devil's Claw, is a flowering plant native to South Africa and the southwestern United States, belonging to the family Pedaliaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, pain, and digestive problems, with reported health benefits including reduced inflammation and improved symptoms of arthritis. The bioactive constituents of Craspedorhachis texana include iridoid glycosides, such as harpagoside, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Devil's Claw include capsules, tablets, and tinctures, often used to alleviate pain and inflammation associated with various conditions, including osteoarthritis and fibromyalgia.
